codeReview.summary: Implementation covers most CRUD behavior and permission checks, but one contract-critical validation is incorrect: PUT accepts decimal order values instead of rejecting non-integers.

issues.description: `PUT /api/admin/categories/:id` treats `order` as any finite number and truncates with `Math.trunc` (e.g., `1.5` -> `1`) instead of rejecting non-integer input. This contradicts VAL-CAT-026 ('order must be integer').
The test named 'returns 400 for non-integer order' only submits `'not-a-number'`; it does not cover decimal input (e.g., `1.5`), so the integer-validation defect is not detected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `fullstack-worker` procedure is over-prescriptive for API-only features and was not followed literally here (tests-first/manual browser steps), yet handoff marks `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requires 'Write API tests first (red)' and 'Manual verification with agent-browser' (`.factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md`), but transcript skeleton shows `Create app/apps/server/src/routes/categories.ts` before creating tests and no agent-browser steps; handoff skillFeedback says followedProcedure true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Category image constraints/location knowledge was rediscovered ad hoc from legacy PHP, suggesting missing codified reference in shared library for categories parity rules.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton includes multiple legacy lookups (`grep -rn "cat_image_path|max_size|allowed_types" .../upload/system/68kb/modules/categories/controllers/admin.php`) before implementation; `.factory/library/` lacks a categories-specific parity note.

summary: Reviewed commit 54f2ef1 and transcript for categories-crud-api. Core endpoints and flows are implemented, but update order validation does not enforce integer-only input, which is a blocking contract miss (VAL-CAT-026). Report marked fail.
